channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Blinken tells AP he's worried Trump administration may abandon key Biden foreign policy initiatives
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Outgoing Secretary of State Antony Blinken told The Associated Press that he hopes the incoming Trump administration will press forward with key points in President Joe Biden’s foreign policy, including on the Middle East and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bribery, fraud charges dropped against former New York Lt. Governor
item.description: Federal prosecutors in New York have dropped bribery and fraud charges against former New York Lt. Gov. Brian Benjamin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yellen says Treasury will use 'extraordinary measures' on Jan. 21 to prevent hitting debt ceiling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In one of her last acts as Treasury Secretary, Janet Yellen said her agency will start taking “extraordinary measures,” or special accounting maneuvers intended to prevent the nation from hitting the debt ceiling, on January 21 in a letter sent to congressional leaders Friday after
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Time's Up for TikTok
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem will '100% partner' with Trump if confirmed as DHS head
item.description: Kristi Noem pledges to work in tandem with Donald Trump if confirmed as the head of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed JD Vance in the US Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted will succeed Vice President-elect JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, the state's governor announced Friday. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayor says his meeting with Trump covered jobs and ceasefire deal, but not his criminal case
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says his meeting with President-elect Donald Trump included discussion of reviving manufacturing jobs in the Bronx and the Gaza ceasefire deal — but not the mayor’s federal bribery charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's swearing-in will move inside the Capitol Rotunda because of intense cold weather
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda on Monday due to forecasts of intense cold weather, upending months of meticulous planning for a massive outdoor event with crowds sprawling down the National Mall.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border Patrol Chief Owens announces retirement, Texas border czar to take over
item.description: Border Patrol Chief Jason Owens is retiring in April, and will be replaced by Texas Border Czar Mike Banks, after a historic crisis at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury Department recoups $31 million in improper government payments to dead people
item.description: The Treasury Department announced this week that it had recouped more than $31 million in fraud and improper payments to dead people during just five months of having access to the Social Security Administration's federal death database.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former CIA analyst pleads guilty to leaking info on planned Israeli attack on Iran
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — A former CIA analyst pleaded guilty Friday to leaking information on a planned Israeli attack on Iran. Asif Rahman, 34, was arrested by the FBI in November weeks after classified documents appeared on the Telegram messaging app.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem optimistic Laken Riley would still be alive if she were DHS secretary, as freshman senator rips Mayorkas
item.description: Trump DHS secretary nominee Kristi Noem told GOP Sen. Bernie Moreno in her confirmation hearing that she believes Laken Riley would be alive if she had been leading DHS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Commerce Secretary Raimondo offers a parting shot at Trump and advice for Democrats' future
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo looks at Donald Trump, she says she sees a president-elect who for all of his aggressive talk is afraid to let America compete with the rest of the world, responding instead with tariffs and curtailing immigration.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says the Equal Rights Amendment should be considered ratified
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that the Equal Rights Amendment should be considered a ratified addition to the U.S. Constitution, making a symbolic statement that’s unlikely to alter a decades-long push for gender equality.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, DARLENE SUPERVILLE and CLAIRE SAVAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump likely to avoid inaugural crowd size controversy with swearing-in moved indoors
item.description: Controversy over President-elect Trump's inaugural crowd sizes likely will remain out of the conversation since the ceremony will be moved indoors due to harsh weather conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2025 Legislature is off to a bumpy start
item.description: DFLers made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo earlier this week as both parties fought over control. Republicans pressed forward anyway. MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the unconventional start to the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: CBO projects US debt to grow $23.9 trillion in 10 years, not including costs of extending tax cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The national debt is slated to rise by $23.9 trillion over the next decade, a sum that does not include trillions of dollars in additional tax cuts being championed by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE caucus plans for biggest impact, eyeing key tools to expedite cutting waste
item.description: The DOGE caucus on Thursday discussed strategic ways to address as much government waste as possible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama wishes wife Michelle happy birthday as she stays away from key public events
item.description: Former President Obama wished his wife, Michelle Obama, a happy birthday on Friday. Michelle has remained out of the spotlight in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Energy expert weighs in on 23 state attorneys general suing EPA over new methane emissions fee
item.description: Steve Milloy, a former Trump administration EPA official, said that Congress needs to remake the law in order for the new methane emissions rule to be reversed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's inauguration will be held inside. What to know if you're traveling to Washington
item.description: The decision to move Monday's swearing-in means thousands of people with plans to visit Washington won’t be able to see President-elect Donald Trump’s second inauguration in person.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gov. Sanders announces plan to empower parents to sue Big Tech for role in teen mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders and Kentucky Gov. Andrew Beshear will be speaking at Davos about economic opportunity, while Sanders will also address Big Tech.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court upholds dismissal of some charges against Trump, others in Georgia election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia appeals court has upheld the dismissal of some of the charges against President-elect Donald Trump and others in the Georgia 2020 election interference case, as the future of the case remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's official X account draws mockery with reference to constitutional amendment that doesn't exist
item.description: President Biden's official X account posted a tweet that seemed to attempt to "declare" the Equal Rights Amendment the 28th amendment to the US Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to be inaugurated inside: Last ceremony held indoors was Reagan's in 1985
item.description: President-elect Trump's inauguration will now take place inside the U.S. Capitol due to cold weather forecast for Monday, the first indoor inauguration since Ronald Reagan's second inauguration in January 1985.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted expected to fill JD Vance's Senate seat
item.description: DeWine is expected to announce Friday that he is selecting Jon Husted to fill the Senate seat vacated by JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: A rebranded Women's March returns before Trump's inauguration as progressives seek to regroup
item.description: When Elisabeth Bramble and her sister stepped off the Washington Metro to join the massive crowds during the 2017 Women’s March, she counted it as one of the most powerful moments of her life. On Saturday, she will board a 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump inauguration moved indoors because of freezing temperatures
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony is being moved indoors because of expected bitter cold Monday in Washington, D.C. Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance are expected to be sworn in inside the Capitol Rotunda rather than on the West Front of the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE eyes suggestions to slash federal DEI programs: report
item.description: DOGE is taking a look at a report that claims there is more than $120 billion annually spent on “diversity, equity and inclusion" initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani’s Florida condo, World Series rings on the line in latest trial
item.description: Rudy Giuliani will again square off in court against two Georgia election workers Thursday, this time seeking to keep his Florida condominium and other prized possessions from the women who won a $146 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada attorney general moves to protect health care access for DACA recipients
item.description: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is intervening to protect access to health care insurance for Dreamers — the recipients of the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) policy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Departing DHS Secretary Mayorkas contends he delivered border security in the end
item.description: As DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas prepares to leave office, NPR sits down for an exit interview. He tells us the border is more secure now than before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara and Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
